member.name: M:WebReaper.Builders.ConfigBuilder.Get(System.String[])
member.summary: This method can be called only one time to specify urls to start crawling with.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Builders.ConfigBuilder.GetWithBrowser(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Collections.Generic.List{WebReaper.Domain.PageActions.PageAction})
member.summary: This method can be called only one time to specify urls to start crawling with.
member.param: Actions to perform on the page via a browser

member.name: M:WebReaper.Builders.ConfigBuilder.StopWhenAllLinksProcessed
member.summary: Stop the engine once every discovered link has been crawled, instead of running forever waiting for new jobs (issue #20). Works with the in-memory scheduler.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Builders.ScraperEngineBuilder
member.summary: Builds a web scraper engine responsible for creating and receiving crawling jobs and running a spider on them

member.name: M:WebReaper.Builders.ScraperEngineBuilder.WithJsonContentParser
member.summary: Parse responses as JSON instead of HTML (issue #27). Schema selectors become JSONPath expressions.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Builders.ScraperEngineBuilder.StopWhenAllLinksProcessed
member.summary: Stop the engine once all discovered links have been crawled (issue #20), so await engine.RunAsync() actually returns for finite crawls. Uses the in-memory scheduler.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Builders.SpiderBuilder.WithJsonContentParser
member.summary: Parse responses as JSON instead of HTML (issue #27). Schema selectors become JSONPath expressions. For a different document shape (e.g. HtmlAgilityPack, XPath), implement and pass new SchemaContentParser<TNode>(backend, logger) to — it reuses the shared Schema fold rather than re-implementing the walk.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Builders.SpiderBuilder.WithPageLoader(WebReaper.Core.Loaders.Abstract.IPageLoader)
member.summary: Supply a custom page loader. By default wires the HTTP and headless-browser transports behind one and passes the optional proxy provider to both (ADR 0004).

member.name: M:WebReaper.Builders.SpiderBuilder.WithValidatedProxies(WebReaper.Proxy.Abstract.IProxySource,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{WebReaper.Proxy.Abstract.IProxyValidator},WebReaper.Proxy.ValidatedProxyProviderOptions)
member.summary: Use proxies from , but only after they pass every supplied validator. Plugs into the same pipeline as .

member.name: T:WebReaper.ConfigStorage.Concrete.FileScraperConfigStorage
member.summary: Source-compatible constructor over the config payload shell backed by a (ADR 0003). The is the blob key, so the config is written to exactly that path — unchanged from before.

member.name: T:WebReaper.ConfigStorage.Concrete.InMemoryScraperConfigStorage
member.summary: Source-compatible constructor over the config payload shell backed by an (ADR 0003).

member.name: T:WebReaper.ConfigStorage.Concrete.MongoDbScraperConfigStorage
member.summary: Source-compatible constructor over the config payload shell backed by a (ADR 0003). The is the blob key (document _id). The parameter is retained for binary/source compatibility; it is no longer used here.

member.name: T:WebReaper.ConfigStorage.Concrete.RedisScraperConfigStorage
member.summary: Source-compatible constructor over the config payload shell backed by a (ADR 0003). The parameter is retained for binary/source compatibility; serialization and missing-value policy now live in the shell, so it is no longer used here.

member.name: T:WebReaper.ConfigStorage.Concrete.ScraperConfigStore
member.summary: The config payload shell (ADR 0003). The one home for scraper-config serialization and the meaning of absent. Owns — applied symmetrically to serialize and deserialize, so the polymorphic PageAction.Parameters (object[]) and the ImmutableQueue<LinkPathSelector> chain round-trip through every backend (Redis was silently lossy with TypeNameHandling.None; the file adapter serialized with Auto but deserialized with defaults). Delegates storage to one ; the store never sees a config.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.CookieStorage.Concrete.CookieStore
member.summary: The cookie payload shell (ADR 0003). The one home for the ↔ quirk: does not JSON round-trip (internal hash tables), so the shell persists the flat and rebuilds the container on read. This quirk is quarantined here and is unreachable from the and any future backend. Absent ⇒ an empty container (what a fresh crawl wants).

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.CookieStorage.Concrete.FileCookieStorage
member.summary: Source-compatible constructor over the payload shell backed by a (ADR 0003). The is the blob key (the file path). The parameter is retained for binary/source compatibility; it is no longer used here.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.CookieStorage.Concrete.InMemoryCookieStorage
member.summary: Source-compatible constructor over the payload shell backed by an (ADR 0003).

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.CookieStorage.Concrete.MongoDbCookieStorage
member.summary: Source-compatible constructor over the payload shell backed by a (ADR 0003). The is the blob key (document _id). The parameter is retained for binary/source compatibility; it is no longer used here. Replaces the previous adapter whose read called .ToJson() on the FindAsync cursor instead of the document — that bug cannot recur (ADR 0003).

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.CookieStorage.Concrete.RedisCookieStorage
member.summary: Source-compatible constructor over the payload shell backed by a (ADR 0003). The parameter is retained for binary/source compatibility; it is no longer used here.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.Abstract.ICrawlStep
member.summary:
The crawl step: the pure, in-process decision mapping a + its already-loaded document + the parsing to exactly one .
No I/O behind this seam — no page loaders, visited-link tracker, sinks, scheduler, or config storage, and no exception used as control flow (the crawl-limit stop stays in the Spider shell). Deterministic in (job, document, schema). Returned Jobs are candidates — not visited-link filtered; the shell does that.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.Abstract.ICrawlStep.StepAsync(WebReaper.Domain.Job,System.String,WebReaper.Domain.Parsing.Schema)
member.param: The Job being crawled. Its selector chain — its length and whether the single remaining selector paginates — fully determines which arm is returned.
The already-loaded page body. The shell owns loading; the step never fetches.
The Schema for a target page; may be null (no-op extraction). Ignored for transit / paginated pages.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.Concrete.CrawlStep
member.summary: The crawl-step decision extracted from the old Spider.CrawlAsync. Holds the page-category dispatch, the selector-chain advance/retain rule, link extraction, content parsing, and child-Job provenance threading behind one pure method. / are internal seams — injected, never surfaced on .

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.CrawlOutcome
member.summary:
The result of a : exactly one of a target page's , a transit page's followed Jobs, or a paginated page's item + next-page Jobs. Never more than one arm; the arm is a total function of the Job's selector chain.
Closed hierarchy — construct only via the factory members; the union is not extensible. See docs/adr/0001-crawl-outcome-closed-sum.md.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.CrawlOutcome.Parsed
member.summary: Target page (empty selector chain): the page was parsed with the Schema. No further Jobs.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.CrawlOutcome.Parsed.#ctor(WebReaper.Sinks.Models.ParsedData)
member.summary: Target page (empty selector chain): the page was parsed with the Schema. No further Jobs.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.CrawlOutcome.Followed
member.summary: Transit page: the head selector was consumed. Every Job carries the advanced (shortened) selector chain. Empty when no links matched.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.CrawlOutcome.Followed.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{WebReaper.Domain.Job})
member.summary: Transit page: the head selector was consumed. Every Job carries the advanced (shortened) selector chain. Empty when no links matched.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.CrawlOutcome.Paginated
member.summary: Page with pagination. are item Jobs that advance — their chain is emptied, so they are target pages. are next-page Jobs that retain the same one-element paginated chain, because page 2 of a listing is the same step, not a deeper one. Either list may be empty.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.CrawlOutcome.Paginated.#ctor(System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{WebReaper.Domain.Job},System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray{WebReaper.Domain.Job})
member.summary: Page with pagination. are item Jobs that advance — their chain is emptied, so they are target pages. are next-page Jobs that retain the same one-element paginated chain, because page 2 of a listing is the same step, not a deeper one. Either list may be empty.

member.name: P:WebReaper.Core.Crawling.CrawlOutcome.NextJobs
member.summary: All candidate next Jobs in deterministic order: , or then , or empty for . Candidates only — visited-link filtering is the caller's job.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Loaders.Abstract.IPageLoader
member.summary: The one page-loading seam (ADR 0004). Turns a into the page's HTML, dispatching on to one . The Spider holds a single and is loader-blind. Replaces the former single-adapter IStaticPageLoader / IBrowserPageLoader pair.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Loaders.Abstract.IPageLoadTransport
member.summary: One page-load mechanism behind the (ADR 0004): HTTP or headless browser. The single home for that mechanism's client / launch quirks and for how it applies the optional proxy. Two real adapters — HttpPageLoadTransport and BrowserPageLoadTransport — so this is a genuine seam, not indirection.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Loaders.Abstract.PageRequest
member.summary: What the page loader needs to fetch one page (ADR 0004): the URL, the load mode, optional browser page actions, and the headless flag. Projected from a Job plus the crawl's headless setting — the loader never sees the selector chain or backlinks. The HTTP transport ignores and ; that is the accepted fat-field cost of having one loading seam instead of two.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Loaders.Abstract.PageRequest.#ctor(System.String,WebReaper.Domain.Selectors.PageType,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{WebReaper.Domain.PageActions.PageAction},System.Boolean)
member.summary: What the page loader needs to fetch one page (ADR 0004): the URL, the load mode, optional browser page actions, and the headless flag. Projected from a Job plus the crawl's headless setting — the loader never sees the selector chain or backlinks. The HTTP transport ignores and ; that is the accepted fat-field cost of having one loading seam instead of two.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Loaders.Concrete.BrowserPageLoadTransport
member.summary: Headless-browser — the one home for the Puppeteer path (ADR 0004). Replaces PuppeteerPageLoader, PuppeteerPageLoaderWithProxies, and the BrowserPageLoader base. The only genuine variation between the old pair — plain Puppeteer vs PuppeteerExtra + stealth + proxy args + AuthenticateAsync — is the single _proxyProvider is not null branch here. Navigation waits on (the old pair diverged between that and DOMContentLoaded by accident; networkidle is correct for the JS-rendered pages this transport exists for).

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Loaders.Concrete.HttpPageLoadTransport
member.summary: HTTP — the one home for the HTTP client / handler (ADR 0004). Replaces HttpStaticPageLoader and the PageRequester / ProxyPageRequester / RotatingProxyPageRequester triad. The handler is built after the cookies are fetched, so stored cookies are actually applied — the bug the old non-proxy path had (handler built in the requester's constructor, cookie container set afterwards). One canonical User-Agent (the triad had two by copy-drift). Client is per-request, mirroring the only correct member of the old triad (the rotating one); this trades connection pooling for correct per-request cookie/proxy application — connection-pool tuning is a separate concern, out of scope for this deepening.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Loaders.Concrete.PageLoader
member.summary: The one : the single home for the load-mode decision the Spider used to make. Dispatches on to the HTTP or browser (ADR 0004).

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Abstract.ISchemaBackend`1
member.summary: One document shape = one backend. Supplies only the four document-primitive operations the fold needs; it does NOT know the grammar (container / object-list / leaf / value-list), type coercion, the missing-node policy, or the swallow-and-log scope — those live once, in the fold. A new backend (HtmlAgilityPack, System.Text.Json, …) is an implementation of this seam, not a re-derivation of the walk.
member.typeparam: The backend's opaque scope cursor — an AngleSharp node, a JToken, etc. The fold never names it; it only threads it back through these methods.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Abstract.ISchemaBackend`1.RootAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Parse a raw document string into its root scope.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Abstract.ISchemaBackend`1.SelectMany(`0,System.String)
member.summary: Every node under matching the selector (list paths).

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Abstract.ISchemaBackend`1.SelectOne(`0,System.String)
member.summary: The first node under matching the selector, or null if none.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Abstract.ISchemaBackend`1.ExtractRaw(`0,WebReaper.Domain.Parsing.SchemaElement)
member.summary: The raw leaf value of for : a for text/markup backends, a native value for structured ones. The fold applies any coercion; an untyped leaf is this value verbatim. Backend-local quirks (e.g. the AngleSharp src→title rewrite) belong here and nowhere else.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Concrete.AngleSharpContentParser
member.summary: HTML content parser: the shared fold over the . Kept as a named type with its original (ILogger) constructor so it stays the default and a drop-in for existing callers.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Concrete.AngleSharpSchemaBackend
member.summary: HTML backend for : AngleSharp DOM, CSS selectors. The scope cursor is (the document at the top, a matched element when recursing). This is the ONLY place the src→title rewrite and the attr/markup/text rules live — the shared fold never sees them.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Concrete.JsonContentParser
member.summary: Parses a JSON response instead of HTML (issue #27 — scraping JSON endpoints such as the WordPress REST API). The shared fold over the : each is a JSONPath expression and works exactly as it does for the HTML parser. Kept as a named type with its original (ILogger) constructor so WithJsonContentParser and existing callers are unaffected.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Concrete.JsonSchemaBackend
member.summary: JSON backend for (issue #27 — scraping JSON endpoints such as the WordPress REST API). The scope cursor is a and each is a JSONPath expression. returns the native token, so an untyped leaf keeps its JSON type (a number stays a number) — the structured-side of the divergence the shared fold passes straight through.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Parser.Concrete.SchemaContentParser`1
member.summary: The single recursive fold, shared by every backend. It owns the grammar the parsers used to each re-implement: the container / object-list / leaf / value-list branching, the coercion, the missing-node → log → empty-string policy, and the swallow-and-log scope (only a leaf assignment is guarded; a container whose list selector is missing still throws, exactly as before). Everything document-shaped is delegated to .
member.remarks: Public and generic on purpose: a new backend is new SchemaContentParser<TNode>(myBackend, logger) passed to WithContentParser, reusing this proven fold rather than copying it. See docs/adr/0002.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Core.Scheduler.Abstract.IScheduler.Complete
member.summary: Signals that no more jobs will ever be added, so can complete and the engine can stop once everything has been crawled (issue #20). Default is a no-op: durable / distributed schedulers keep their long-running behavior unless they choose to override this.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Core.Spider.Concrete.Spider
member.summary: The I/O shell around the . It owns everything the crawl step deliberately does not: page loading, the visited-link tracker, the crawl-limit stop, sink fan-out, the PostProcessor / ScrapedData event, and adapting the back to the engine's List<Job> contract. The crawl-vs-parse decision itself lives behind .

member.name: T:WebReaper.DataAccess.FileBlobStore
member.summary: File-backed . The key is the file path — this preserves the historical new FileScraperConfigStorage("config.json") behaviour of writing to exactly that file. UTF-8, no BOM (the default).

member.name: T:WebReaper.DataAccess.IKeyedBlobStore
member.summary: Backend-agnostic persistence seam: stores and fetches one opaque UTF-8 string under a caller-supplied key. is upsert/replace (last write wins, never append). A null result from means the key is absent — absence is not an error at this seam; what absence means is decided by the payload shell above it (see , ). The key is opaque to the store and scoped to the backend the adapter talks to (a file path, a Redis key, a document id). The store never knows which payload it holds. See docs/adr/0003-keyed-blob-store-and-payload-shells.md.

member.name: M:WebReaper.DataAccess.IKeyedBlobStore.PutAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Persist under , replacing any existing value.

member.name: M:WebReaper.DataAccess.IKeyedBlobStore.GetAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Fetch the value stored under , or null if the key is absent.

member.name: T:WebReaper.DataAccess.InMemoryBlobStore
member.summary: In-process . Holds the serialized blob, not the live object — so the in-memory path exercises the same payload-shell serialization the persistent backends do (ADR 0003).

member.name: T:WebReaper.DataAccess.MongoBlobStore
member.summary: MongoDB-backed . Stores an opaque { _id: key, blob: value } document — WebReaper only ever fetches a whole payload by key and never queries inside it, so the historical queryable-BSON projection was never load-bearing (ADR 0003). The upsert makes the old InsertOneAsync + FirstOrDefault append-and-read-oldest bug unrepresentable.

member.name: T:WebReaper.DataAccess.RedisBlobStore
member.summary: Redis-backed . Owns one multiplexer per distinct connection string (the pattern StackExchange.Redis recommends), replacing RedisBase's process-static, first-connection- wins multiplexer that silently ignored every connection string after the first — a latent bug for the distributed mode the README sells (ADR 0003).

member.name: P:WebReaper.Domain.Parsing.SchemaElement.IsList
member.summary: When true, every node matching is extracted into a JSON array instead of just the first match. For a element this yields an array of objects (child selectors evaluated relative to each match); for a leaf element it yields an array of values. Issue #28.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Exceptions.ConfigNotFoundException
member.summary: Thrown by the config payload shell when the scraper config is absent. The uniform missing-value policy (ADR 0003): absence of a config is unrecoverable — CreateConfigAsync must run before GetConfigAsync. Replaces the file adapter's bare and the other backends' silent null.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Extensions.CookieExtensions.ToPuppeteerCookies(System.Net.CookieContainer,System.String)
member.summary:
Maps a to Puppeteer s for the given target URL.
Fixes issue #26: previously only Name/Value were copied and cookies were set on a blank page before navigation, so Puppeteer had no domain to attach them to and the login was lost. We now carry Domain/Path/Secure/HttpOnly/Expires, and fall back to the target when a cookie has no domain so it can be set before the first navigation.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Proxy.Abstract.IProxySource
member.summary: Produces the full set of candidate proxies (before validation). Implemented by anything that knows where proxies come from: a paid proxy API, a static list, a scraped free-proxy page, etc.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Proxy.Abstract.IProxySource.GetCandidatesAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns the current list of candidate proxies. Called repeatedly by on every refresh, so implementations should be cheap or cache.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Proxy.Abstract.IProxyValidator
member.summary: Decides whether a single candidate proxy is usable. Multiple validators can be combined; a proxy is kept only if every validator approves it (logical AND).

member.name: M:WebReaper.Proxy.Abstract.IProxyValidator.IsValidAsync(System.Net.WebProxy,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Returns true if the proxy passes this validator's check. Implementations must not throw for an unusable proxy: catch the failure and return false. Honor .

member.name: T:WebReaper.Proxy.Concrete.HttpProxyValidator
member.summary:
Validates a proxy by issuing a real HTTP request through it and checking the response succeeds.
This deliberately does NOT use ICMP ping: ping proves the host is up, not that it forwards HTTP traffic, and many proxies/firewalls drop ICMP entirely. An actual request through the proxy is the only check that means "this proxy works for scraping".

member.name: M:WebReaper.Proxy.Concrete.HttpProxyValidator.#ctor(System.String)
member.param: URL fetched through the proxy. Default is Google's connectivity endpoint, which returns an empty 204 quickly.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Proxy.Concrete.StaticProxySource
member.summary: A fixed set of proxies known up front (e.g. from config or a paid plan). The list is captured once at construction.

member.name: M:WebReaper.Proxy.Concrete.StaticProxySource.FromAddresses(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})
member.summary: Build a source from scheme://host:port strings, e.g. http://1.2.3.4:8080. Scheme defaults to http if omitted.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Proxy.Concrete.ValidatedProxyProvider
member.summary:
An that only ever hands out proxies that passed validation. It pulls candidates from an , keeps the ones every approves, caches that set, and re-validates lazily once it goes stale.
Being an , it drops into the existing pipeline (SpiderBuilder.WithProxies → page requesters / Puppeteer loader) with no other changes. No background service, no extra hosting dependency: refresh happens on demand, single-flighted.

member.name: T:WebReaper.Proxy.ValidatedProxyProviderOptions
member.summary: Tuning knobs for .

member.name: P:WebReaper.Proxy.ValidatedProxyProviderOptions.RefreshInterval
member.summary: How long a validated list is reused before the source is queried and proxies are re-validated. Default: 5 minutes.

member.name: P:WebReaper.Proxy.ValidatedProxyProviderOptions.ValidationTimeout
member.summary: Per-proxy timeout for a single validator. A validator that exceeds this is treated as a failed (invalid) proxy. Default: 10 seconds.

member.name: P:WebReaper.Proxy.ValidatedProxyProviderOptions.MaxConcurrentValidations
member.summary: Maximum number of proxies validated concurrently. Default: 20.
